identity.short_name: Linear congruence with split valuations, order lifted to $q^2$, and Hensel square-root coupling

problem_spec.summary: Solve a coupled linear congruence $ax\equiv b\pmod{n}$ with $n=p^kq$ where $A=(a^{q-1}-1)/p^k$ and $C=(a^{p-1}-1)/q$ are defined in an exact-valuation regime forced by LTE. Compute $x$ by determining $x\bmod q$ and $x\bmod p^k$, recombining by CRT, then compute $t=\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(x)$ by lifting the order from $q$ to $q^2$. Finally compute a uniquely selected Hensel-lifted square root $y$ of $x+(p-1)$ modulo $p^k$, and output the normalized residue $N\equiv x+t+y\pmod{999}$.
givens: $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes, $k\ge 5$, and $n=p^kq$.
$A=(a^{q-1}-1)/p^k$ and $C=(a^{p-1}-1)/q$ are integers (forced by the valuation constraints).
$x$ is the least nonnegative solution to $ax\equiv b\pmod n$.
$y$ is the unique integer with $0\le y<p^k$ satisfying $y^2\equiv x+(p-1)\pmod{p^k}$ and $y\equiv 1\pmod p$.
$N$ is the unique integer with $0\le N<999$ and $N\equiv x+t+y\pmod{999}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: base prime for the $p$-power modulus and $p$-adic expansions

parameter(s).name: k
parameter(s).purpose: size of the $p$-power modulus $p^k$ and the coupling exponent $p^{k-4}$

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: near-$1$ unit enabling LTE, binomial truncation, and controlled residue computations

invariant(s).name: hensel_square_shift_selector
invariant(s).statement: The constraints $y^2\equiv x+(p-1)\pmod{p^k}$ and $y\equiv 1\pmod p$ uniquely determine $y$ by Hensel lifting in the simple-root regime.

essential_assumption(s): $\gcd(a,n)=1$ so the linear congruence has a unique solution class modulo $n$.
$y\equiv 1\pmod p$ selects the unique Hensel lift among the two square roots.
The reported output is the canonical residue $N$ with $0\le N<999$ and $N\equiv x+t+y\pmod{999}$.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Since $\gcd(a,n)=1$, the congruence $ax\equiv b\pmod n$ has a unique solution class modulo $n$, and the normalization $0\le x<n$ fixes a unique $x$. The order $t=\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(x)$ is uniquely defined because $\gcd(x,q)=1$, and the order-lifting gate $x^{\operatorname{ord}_q(x)}\not\equiv 1\pmod{q^2}$ forces $t=q\operatorname{ord}_q(x)$. The auxiliary variable $y$ is uniquely determined by Hensel's lemma in the simple-root regime together with the selector $y\equiv 1\pmod p$. Finally $N$ is uniquely determined by the normalization $0\le N<999$ and $N\equiv x+t+y\pmod{999}$.

technique(s).name: Lifting_the_Exponent_LTE
inputs: $a\equiv 1\pmod p$ and $a\equiv 1\pmod q$ with controlled valuations
technique(s).purpose: prove $A,C\in\mathbb{Z}$ and obtain exact (or sufficient) valuations
outputs: integrality and nondivisibility information for $A$ and $C$

technique(s).name: binomial_expansion_truncation
inputs: expansions of $(1+p^eu)^{q-1}$ and $(1+qm)^{p-1}$ modulo prime powers

technique(s).name: Kummer_carry_count
technique(s).purpose: alternate justification for binomial valuations when convenient

technique(s).name: Euclidean_algorithm_and_Bezout
inputs: pairs of integers for modular inverses
technique(s).purpose: compute inverses modulo $p^k$ and solve linear congruences

technique(s).name: Euler_criterion_and_quadratic_reciprocity
technique(s).purpose: force $u^{(q-1)/2}\equiv -1\pmod q$ when needed in order tests

technique(s).name: Hensel_lifting_square_root
inputs: a simple root modulo $p$ for $Y^2-s$ and a selector $Y\equiv y_0\pmod p$

theorem(s).name: gcd_condition_for_linear_congruence
theorem(s).role: if $\gcd(a,n)=1$ then $ax\equiv b\pmod n$ has a unique solution class

theorem(s).name: Binomial_theorem
theorem(s).role: expands near-$1$ powers and allows truncation modulo prime powers

theorem(s).name: Legendre_formula
theorem(s).role: computes $v_p(n!)$ and hence valuations of binomial coefficients

theorem(s).name: Euler_criterion
theorem(s).role: relates $u^{(q-1)/2}\bmod q$ to the Legendre symbol $(u/q)$

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root
theorem(s).role: lifts a simple root modulo $p$ uniquely to a root modulo $p^k$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: p-adic valuation controls divisibility and truncation of expansions modulo $p^k$
orders in finite groups lift through group extensions with controlled kernel size
Hensel lifting is $p$-adic Newton iteration for polynomial congruences

critical_trick(s): split valuations ensure $A$ is an integer and computable modulo $p^k$
order-lifting gate determines whether the order jumps from $q$ to $q^2$
Hensel selector $y\equiv 1\pmod p$ guarantees uniqueness of the lifted square root

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: gcd checks and modular arithmetic sanity checks

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: modular inverses, multiplicative order computation, and exact modular verification

task(s).name: end_to_end_verification
task(s).reason: check that $A,C$ are integers, $ax\equiv b\pmod n$, compute $t=\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(x)$, verify $y$, and compute $N$
